reforms.description: The Government of Japan adopted a major overhaul of pension provisions, reducing the full pension due in the early 21st century and increasing the standard contribution period.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to stabilize the pension system but may have mixed effects on household savings and consumption.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms were approved by the Diet in April 1985.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in Japan's pension system, addressing long-term sustainability and fiscal health.
reforms.source_quote: The Government has adopted a major overhaul of pension provisions: the reforms were approved by the Diet in April 1985.

reforms.description: The 'Temporary Measures Law for Structural Improvement of Specific Industries' was enacted to assist in the elimination of excess capacity and promote the development and application of new technology in 26 industries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to enhance technological development and application, which is expected to improve productivity and efficiency in targeted industries.
reforms.status_evidence: The law has been enacted and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change aimed at improving industry capacity and technology application.
reforms.source_quote: New legislation (the 'Temporary Measures Law for Structural Improvement of Specific Industries') was therefore put in place.

reforms.description: The System of Subsidies for Promoting the Development of Computers was adopted, providing conditional loans to support the development costs of new computer technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support for computer technology development, the reform is expected to stimulate innovation and enhance competitiveness in the tech sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The system has been adopted but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant as it provides targeted funding to support a key area of technological advancement.
reforms.source_quote: In 1971, the System of Subsidies for Promoting the Development of Computers was adopted: a 50 percent conditional loan was provided for the development costs of the new machines.
